pattern: After sorting the robots, each gap between neighbors is contested only by the left robot firing right and the right robot firing left, so a small left-to-right DP over each robot's chosen direction maximizes the union of destroyed walls.
hints: A bullet is stopped by the next robot, so a robot only ever reaches walls in the gap immediately beside it.
Sort robots by position; the walls between two adjacent robots can only be hit by the left one shooting right or the right one shooting left, and each wall counts once.

Run a DP indexed by robot with a state for its firing direction; the contribution of the gap to a robot's left depends jointly on that robot firing left and the previous robot firing right (union of their reaches), and walls sitting exactly on a robot are always destroyed.

edge_inputs.description: single robot reaches one of two walls
a robot blocks another's shot, zero destroyed
wall sitting exactly on a robot position
two robots compete for the same middle walls
robots given out of sorted order

stress.description: random small robot and wall sets with unique positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 11))
    coords = rng.sample(range(1, 60), min(2 * n, 59))
    robots = coords[:n]
    distance = [rng.randrange(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    wcount = rng.randrange(1, 12)
    pool = rng.sample(range(1, 60), wcount)
    walls = pool
    return [robots, distance, walls]
stress.note: kept to <=11 robots so the 2^n direction-brute oracle stays fast
